paper_evidence.summary: BRAF V600E-positive MM patients had higher incidence of developing extramedullary disease: 4/7 (57%) vs 43/251 (17%); P=0.02.
paper_evidence.location: Results; Table 1 / Figure 2 narrative (page showing cohort comparison).

fields.evidence_description: Higher incidence of extramedullary disease: 4/7 vs 43/251; P=0.02.
fields.verbatim_quote: Remarkably, four of the seven BRAF-mutated patients (57%) went on to develop extramedullary disease compared with 43 of 251 (17%, P = 0.02) control patients with symptomatic disease.

ai_items.notes: Core claim and statistics match the paper. Cohort sizing is broadly correct (379 total), but the exact denominator for the control comparison is 251 symptomatic MM.

paper_evidence.summary: BRAF V600E carriers had shorter median overall survival than non-carriers, assessed among patients with ≥3 months follow-up from start of first-line therapy.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Median OS 45 months (range 6–54) vs 105 months (range 4–227); P=0.04.

fields.evidence_description: Median OS 45 months vs 105 months; P=0.04; follow-up ≥3 months from first-line start.
fields.verbatim_quote: Those patients confirmed to have the BRAF V600E mutation were found to have a significantly shorter median overall survival of 45 months (range 6 to 54) as compared with 105 months (range 4 to 227 months; P = 0.04) in patients without the mutation.

ai_items.notes: Matches the paper’s OS comparison and correctly includes the follow-up constraint.

fields.evidence_description: BRAF V600E had shorter OS than wild-type.
fields.source_title: Targeting the BRAF V600E mutation in multiple myeloma.

gt_items.notes: Ground truth correctly captures the OS direction/significance but is less quantitative/complete than the paper and AI.

paper_evidence.summary: Case report (patient #5): off-label low-dose vemurafenib produced rapid shrinkage of extramedullary plasmacytomas and IMWG partial response by day 28; response durability reported with ongoing therapy. Paper also reports treatment interruption due to pneumonia and other adverse symptoms (arthralgia/bursitis-like pain, tremor) and notes no acanthomas/SCC at reporting time.
paper_evidence.location: Results + Figure 3; follow-up paragraph describing dosing, response metrics, and adverse events.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Sum of diameters 14.5×5.7 cm → 6.4×1.7 cm (day 28). Light-chain proteinuria decreased; IMWG partial response; durable course reported.

fields.evidence_description: Partial response with rapid regression within 2 weeks; lesion diameters decreased at day 28; durable after ~8 months.
fields.verbatim_quote: Within 2 weeks, the soft tissue plasmacytomas visually reduced in size... Whole-body MRI scan and measurements of the three reference skin lesions showed a decrease...

ai_items.notes: Core response claim matches the paper, but the AI omits paper-reported adverse events/interruptions and imports a FAERS/OpenFDA 'drug_safety' block that is not supported by the paper text.

missing_from_ai: Paper-reported on-treatment adverse events and interruption details in the vemurafenib case (e.g., arthralgia/bursitis-like pain, tremor, pneumonia-related discontinuation, and explicit 'no acanthomas/SCC' statement at reporting time).
ai_hallucinated_items: FAERS/OpenFDA-derived 'drug_safety.top_adverse_events' block is not present in the paper and should not be treated as paper-supported evidence.
ai_improvements_over_gt: AI captures extramedullary disease risk association (absent from GT).
AI captures vemurafenib therapeutic response case report (absent from GT).
AI includes quantitative OS and extramedullary statistics more explicitly than GT.

overall_judgement.summary: AI extraction is high precision for the core clinical claims and materially more complete than the ground truth, but it incorrectly treats an external FAERS/OpenFDA safety summary as if it were part of the paper record and it misses the paper’s actual case-level adverse event details.
strengths: Correct identification of the key biomarker (BRAF V600E) and disease context (MM).
Accurate capture of the two cohort-based prognostic associations with correct statistics and P-values.
Accurate capture of the vemurafenib response signal (case report) with key tumor-measurement numbers.
weaknesses: Safety reporting mismatch: misses paper-reported patient adverse events while adding non-paper FAERS/OpenFDA events.
Some fields are overconfidently populated without paper-confirmed specificity (e.g., some identifier/variant-origin assumptions are reasonable but not explicitly asserted in-text).
